articles.title: A Library Dedicated Solely to the Epstein Files Is Opening in New York
articles.description: The Institute for Primary Facts has compiled more than 3.5 million pages of the Epstein files for public display at the newly opened Donald J. Trump and Jeffrey Epstein Memorial Reading Room.

articles.title: The brain's hidden defense against a potentially harmful metal
articles.description: Researchers identify a molecular gatekeeper that clears excess manganese from the brain, offering new clues about a rare neurological condition.

articles.title: Ordinary People Fear AI, While the Tech Leaders Working to Create a Permanent Underclass Say They’re Extremely Psyched About It
articles.description: A major gulf in expectations. The post Ordinary People Fear AI, While the Tech Leaders Working to Create a Permanent Underclass Say They’re Extremely Psyched About It appeared first on Futurism .

articles.title: Neandertals used rhinoceros teeth as tools
articles.description: Finds at sites in Spain and France suggest that Neandertals used the teeth of ancient rhinos for heavy-duty fabrication.

articles.title: After 40 Years, Venture Investors Rebrands as Clarevia Ventures, Marking a Clearer Identity for Its Next Chapter in Healthcare Investing
articles.description: MADISON, Wis.--(BUSINESS WIRE)--Venture Investors Health Fund, one of the Midwest’s longest-standing healthcare-focused venture firms, today announced its rebrand to Clarevia Ventures, a new name that reflects the firm’s sharpened focus on helping early-stage medtech and life sciences companies navigate the path from breakthrough science to patient impact. The rebrand comes after years of evolution within the firm. While Clarevia has invested exclusively in healthcare since 2018, the new identi

articles.title: SIM swap scam drained Florida woman's bank account in minutes
articles.description: SIM swap scams let hackers steal your phone number and drain accounts in minutes. Learn how one woman was targeted and how to protect yourself.

articles.description: 'Hundreds' of salmon may have died at Tievenny Burn in County Tyrone, anglers say.

articles.title: A Landslide in Alaska Set Off a Tsunami. There May Be More to Come.
articles.description: Scientists say as glaciers retreat in a warming climate, landslide-generated tsunamis are likely to become more frequent.

articles.title: Opinion: Trump’s executive order on psychedelics is the right move. But is my field ready for it?
articles.description: The psychedelics field is getting what it has long wanted. Now it has to step up.

articles.title: STAT+: Even at a meeting in Rome, FDA shifts are top of mind for gene therapy field
articles.description: Cell and gene therapy makers met in Rome to discuss the field's outlook in Europe. One main topic of conversation: the FDA.

articles.title: Study reveals: Processed food causes fat accumulation in muscle
articles.description: The findings indicate a possible impairment in muscle function and joint health, regardless of body weight or level of physical activity.

articles.title: Nordic Climate Group publishes Sustainability Report for 2025
articles.description: Nordic Climate Group publishes its Sustainability Report for 2025.

articles.title: Scientists may have just found the key reason why coffee is good for you
articles.description: Antioxidants and anti-inflammatory chemicals in coffee may be targeting a specific protein

articles.title: Megafires may drive the prairie grouse into sub-optimal habitats
articles.description: Grasslands and associated wildlife in the Great Plains of North America have declined precipitously and are now experiencing an increase in large wildfire activity. In a Journal of Wildlife Management study evaluating habitat use by lesser prairie chickens—a prairie grouse of conservation concern—before and immediately after a 2017 megafire, investigators found that the birds were forced out of formerly high-quality habitat in large, contiguous grasslands and into sub-optimal habitat and smaller grassland patches near cropland.

articles.title: PEG400 reveals parasite-only pocket that could sharpen malaria treatment
articles.description: Research published in The FEBS Journal may help overcome challenges to the treatment of malaria—a tropical disease caused by infection of red blood cells with Plasmodium parasites, which are transmitted through infected mosquito bites. The research is based on a strategy that targets an enzyme specific to the parasite, falcipain-2 (FP2), which is essential for parasite survival and growth within the host.

articles.title: 'Fire weather' contributing to longer wildfire seasons, say scientists
articles.description: Figures show that spring drought events are happening more often while there has been a sharp rise in "fire weather".

articles.title: Beavers spotted in nature reserve for first time
articles.description: The pair are filmed swimming in the water at Blashford Lakes Nature Reserve, near Ringwood.

articles.title: Once and for all: Which burns more calories – walking or running?
articles.description: Both incline walking and running are considered effective aerobic workouts, but the choice between them depends on fitness goals, health condition, and the ability to maintain consistency over time.

articles.title: Le télescope Major Atmospheric Gamma - ray Imaging Cherenkov (« Magic »), situé à l'observatoire du Roque de los Muchachos de La Palma, aux Canaries.
articles.description: La PME de Lannion, dans les Côtes-d'Armor, spécialisée dans les traitements de surface optiques, va produire 700 miroirs pour un observatoire international chilien.

articles.title: Sheriff Bianco rejects claims he said Hilton was trying to swindle into election
articles.description: Republican candidate Chad Bianco appeared to backpedal after he was asked about a quote from an interview he did with KBAK in March that the other GOP contender, Steve Hilton, “swindled his way into the Republican side, even though he’s not.”

articles.title: Space junk falls back to Earth faster as sunspot numbers climb
articles.description: A new study links the sun's 11-year cycle to accelerated orbital loss, with debris falling faster once sunspot numbers near their cycle peak.

articles.title: Genetic background shapes outcomes in chromosome 16 deletion disorders
articles.description: Individuals that share the same deletion of a portion of chromosome 16 are at risk of developing neurodevelopmental disorders, but some experience severe intellectual disability or developmental delay, while others may only exhibit milder psychiatric features like depression or anxiety.

articles.title: Hantavirus: The rare virus infecting passengers on luxury cruise ship Hondius
articles.description: As of Tuesday, the World Health Organization reported that seven passengers aboard the Hondius were sick or suspected of having contracted Hantavirus, including two confirmed cases.

articles.title: ‘The Beautiful Dome’: Vancouver’s Science World to get FIFA-inspired makeover
articles.description: Work is underway to turn a spherical Vancouver landmark into a massive replica of the official match ball for the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

articles.title: 24th AstroDay sparks keiki science dreams
articles.description: More than 30 organizations came together to offer hands-on astronomy, robotics and live science activities for Hawaiʻi Island families. The post 24th AstroDay sparks keiki science dreams first appeared on University of Hawaiʻi System News .

articles.title: Quantum computers simulate 12,000-atom proteins using 94 qubits in milestone
articles.description: Quantum computers simulate 12,000-atom proteins, marking a major leap in real-world chemistry modeling.

articles.title: Dead orbit: Researchers found a galaxy that isn't spinning — that's not normal
articles.description: Researchers have made a surprising discovery, with the help of the James Webb Space Telescope: a relatively young galaxy that isn’t spinning.

articles.title: State of California Indirect Cost Rate

articles.title: UC Names Katherine Yelick to Head Lawrence Berkeley National Laboratory
articles.description: The post UC Names Katherine Yelick to Head Lawrence Berkeley National Laboratory appeared first on Berkeley Lab News Center .

articles.title: Extinct relative of koalas discovered in Western Australia
articles.description: Fossils reveal that there were at least two kinds of koala when humans first arrived in Australia, but one died out about 30,000 years ago when the west of the continent dried out

articles.title: CPR simulator for space use tracks the differences of blood flow in reduced gravity
articles.description: The new focus on manned missions to the moon and Mars presents countless pressing challenges, including keeping humans alive in hostile environments. What happens when an astronaut or space tourist has a cardiac emergency millions of miles from the nearest hospital?

articles.title: New drug discovered at USask could change cattle breeding worldwide
articles.description: University of Saskatchewan researchers have discovered something they say will revolutionize cattle breeding: an IVF hormone made for humans may have a...

articles.title: Princess of Wales to mark major milestone with official foreign trip
articles.description: The future queen will travel to northern Italy next week on a fact-finding mission for her early years development research centre with her return to travel marking a major mileston

articles.title: How Ancient Centipede Ancestors Conquered the Earth
articles.description: A long-neglected fossil seems to show the evolutionary leap that let the ancestors of today’s many-legged arthropods crawl forth from the seas.

articles.title: Singing mice puff up air sacs to make their sweet songs
articles.description: To serenade with their high-pitched songs, singing mice inflate a throat sac — a use for air sacs seemingly unknown in any other animal.

articles.title: Religious leaders told 'prepare now' for UFO disclosure to unleash Bible-changing revelations
articles.description: Influential Christian leaders have claimed they were part of a shocking meeting with the US government as the world prepares to see the UFO files President Trump promised to release.

articles.title: Dalhousie University scientist using AI for breast cancer research
articles.description: A Dalhousie University is using artificial intelligence to enhance her breast cancer research.

articles.title: How to equip girls for an increasingly AI-driven world
articles.description: Schools must do more to help girls master AI. That's the conclusion of a new study, which found that boys, more confident at working with AI, performed better in some classes compared to their female counterparts.

articles.title: Telehealth Abortion Is Still Possible Without Mifepristone
articles.description: Courts may restrict access to the popular abortion medication mifepristone in the United States. Telehealth providers have backup plans in place.

articles.title: Mexico City is sinking up to 14 inches per year, satellite images show
articles.description: Mexico City is one of the fastest subsiding cities in the world, dropping by up to 14 inches every year.

articles.title: Scientists connect “time crystal” to real device in quantum breakthrough
articles.description: A strange kind of matter that “ticks” forever without energy input has just taken a major leap toward real-world use. Known as a time crystal, this quantum system repeats its motion endlessly—like a clock that never winds down—and scientists have now managed to connect it to an external device for the first time. By linking the time crystal to a tiny mechanical oscillator, researchers showed they can actually control its behavior, opening the door to powerful new technologies.

articles.title: This New Advanced Method Produces Highly Realistic Simulations of Fluid Dynamics
articles.description: Computer scientists at the University of California San Diego have developed a groundbreaking method for generating highly realistic computer-generated images of fluid dynamics, in elements such as smoke.

articles.title: This ‘Living’ Plastic Comes With a Built-in Kill Switch
articles.description: Proof-of-concept tests found that the plastic did such a good job at eating itself apart, not even microplastics had a chance.

articles.title: Warming seas can threaten the hidden relationship that supports seagrass meadows
articles.description: Seagrasses are often overlooked, but they are among the most important coastal habitats on Earth.

articles.title: Carbon dioxide levels in the atmosphere just hit a ‘depressing’ record high
articles.description: These data come from the National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration’s Mauna Loa Observatory, which may soon be shut down because of proposed government budget cuts

articles.title: Breakthrough biomaterial heals tissue from the inside out
articles.description: Scientists have developed a breakthrough injectable biomaterial that travels through the bloodstream to repair damaged tissue from within, reducing inflammation and jumpstarting healing. In animal studies, it successfully treated heart attack damage and even showed promise for conditions like traumatic brain injury and pulmonary hypertension. Unlike earlier approaches that required direct injection into the heart, this new therapy can be delivered intravenously, allowing it to spread evenly and act quickly.

articles.title: Why Blake Lively is trending
articles.description: The high-profile legal battle between Blake Lively and Justin Baldoni’s production company will no longer go to trial after the parties announced a settlement just two weeks before jury selection was slated to begin. CNN’s Jean Casarez breaks down the legal process that led to the settlement. Discover more videos, including live streams and exclusive storytelling, on our Watch page.

articles.title: OHSU-developed blood test could help spot one of the most deadly cancers early
articles.description: OHSU scientists developed a microchip that looks for tumor DNA and proteins in blood, potentially replacing invasive biopsies with a 10‐minute test.
